commentary_text: The market assigns Netherlands a 87% win probability versus the model's 70%, a 17-percentage-point gap favoring the favorite. This sharpening of conviction could reflect recent form, lineup availability, or injury status that the model cannot access. The model sees meaningful draw and Tunisia-win probabilities that the market has compressed substantially, suggesting sportsbooks and traders may have information about team condition or personnel that narrows the competitive range. The dashboard surfaces this as a signal worth monitoring—stronger market confidence in a Netherlands victory—without adjudicating whether that confidence is justified.
